honoree.name: Frank Lloyd Wright
honoree.summary: Frank Lloyd Wright was an American architect born on June 8, 1867, in Richland Center, Wisconsin, and died on April 9, 1959. He is widely regarded as one of the greatest architects of the 20th century, pioneering the 'organic architecture' movement and designing over 1,000 structures, including iconic works like Fallingwater and the Guggenheim Museum. Wright began his career under Louis Sullivan in Chicago and later founded his own practice, influencing modern architecture globally. His innovative designs emphasized harmony between human habitation and the natural environment, leaving a lasting legacy in architectural history.

honoree.name: Gaylord Nelson
honoree.summary: Gaylord Nelson was born on June 4, 1916, in Clear Lake, Wisconsin, and became a prominent American politician and environmentalist. He served as a U.S. Senator from Wisconsin (1963–1981) and was the founder of Earth Day, first celebrated in 1970, which significantly raised global awareness about environmental issues. Prior to his Senate career, he was the 35th Governor of Wisconsin (1959–1963) and a state legislator, advocating for progressive policies like conservation and public education. Nelson died on July 3, 2005, leaving a legacy as a pioneering figure in the modern environmental movement.
honoree.education: San Jose State University, Bachelor of Arts (1939), University of Wisconsin Law School, Law Degree (1942)

honoree.name: Iron Brigade
honoree.summary: This content describes Iron Brigade, a video game developed by Double Fine Productions and released in 2011, not a biographical subject. The game is a third-person shooter with tower defense elements, set in an alternate 1910s where mechs battle invading Martians. It received praise for its unique art style, cooperative gameplay, and expansion Rise of the Martian Bear. As this is not about a person, no biographical details like birth, death, or personal achievements apply.

honoree.name: 173rd Airborne Brigade
honoree.summary: The 173rd Airborne Brigade is a United States Army airborne infantry brigade combat team based in Vicenza, Italy. Originally activated in 1915 as an infantry brigade, it was later re-designated as an airborne unit in 1963 and became famous for its combat operations during the Vietnam War, including the Battle of Dak To. Known as the 'Sky Soldiers,' the brigade has participated in conflicts from World War I to the Global War on Terrorism, earning numerous commendations for valor and service. It remains an active and elite rapid-deployment force within the U.S. Army Europe and Africa command structure.

honoree.name: Peace
honoree.summary: The surname 'Peace' is of English origin, derived from the Middle English word 'pees' meaning harmony or tranquility. It was historically used as a nickname for peaceful individuals or as an occupational name for those who enforced peace. Notable bearers include athletes, politicians, and public figures across various fields. The name has spread globally, particularly in English-speaking countries, with variations in spelling and pronunciation over time.

honoree.name: Stephen Ambrose
honoree.summary: Steve Sarkisian is an American football coach and former quarterback born on March 8, 1974. He played college football at Brigham Young University (BYU) and briefly in the Canadian Football League before transitioning into coaching. Sarkisian gained prominence as the head coach at the University of Washington (2009–2013) and later at the University of Southern California (USC), where he also served as offensive coordinator. Currently, he is recognized for his role as the offensive coordinator for the University of Texas, contributing to its football program's success and innovation in offensive strategies.
honoree.education: El Camino College, Brigham Young University (BYU), Bachelors Degree
